title: Cerebras Leads Crop of IPOs Rushing to Tap Market Before SpaceX
summary: A cluster of companies led by artificial-intelligence chipmaker Cerebras Systems Inc. started formally marketing their US IPOs Monday, as firms weighing second-quarter debuts look to get ahead of SpaceX’s potentially record-breaking listing.

title: GameStop Makes $56 Billion Bid for EBay, Four Times Its Size
summary: GameStop Corp. is trying to buy eBay Inc. for about $56 billion in cash and stock, a bold attempt by Ryan Cohen to take over a storied e-commerce name several times larger.

title: United Jet Hits Pole, Truck During Newark Landing Approach
summary: A United Airlines Holdings Inc. plane grazed a vehicle and light post on an adjacent highway in its final approach to Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday, the latest in a string of incidents that have put the spotlight on aviation safety in the US.

title: Choppin’ It Up: SignalFlare’s Lukianoff on Gas, Fast Casual, AI
summary: Fast-casual chains are most exposed to the gas-price spike as their menu prices have crept up to rival full-service, SignalFlare.ai’s founder and CEO Michael Lukianoff tells Bloomberg Intelligence. In this episode of the Choppin’ It Up podcast, Lukianoff sits down with BI’s senior restaurant and foodservice analyst Michael Halen to chat about the impact that higher gasoline prices and inflation will have on the restaurant industry. He also discusses menu management, population growth and, of cou

title: Geothermal Developer Fervo Seeks $1.33 Billion in US IPO
summary: Fervo Energy Co., a geothermal energy developer, is looking to raise as much as $1.33 billion in a US initial public offering, as more firms seek to capitalize on the growing power demand for data centers.

title: Permian Landowner EagleRock Seeks to Raise $346 Million in IPO
summary: EagleRock Land LLC, a Permian Basin landowner and management company, is seeking to raise as much as $346 million in a US initial public offering.

title: Nvidia Rival Cerebras Seeks to Raise $3.5 Billion in US IPO
summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $3.5 billion in an initial public offering, as the artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator looks to counter rivals in the red-hot sector.

title: Blackstone Data Center REIT Seeks to Raise $1.75 Billion in IPO
summary: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, offering investors the chance to capitalize on a surge in building artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Immune System Biotech Odyssey Seeks $238.3 Million in US IPO
summary: Odyssey Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology firm focused on autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, is seeking to raise $238.3 million in an initial public offering.

title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises on Mideast Tension Flare-Up: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell from all-time highs as oil climbed on concerns that heightened Middle East tensions could threaten a fragile ceasefire, keeping energy costs elevated while fueling inflation risks.

title: Some Extraordinary Numbers From Earnings Season
summary: Another line that’s going up.

title: John Zito on Financial Markets, Investment Strategies, AI
summary: Apollo Asset Management Co-President John Zito discusses financial market conditions and investment strategies around the growth of AI. He speaks with Dani Burger at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PJT CEO Says Retail Will Stop Fueling Private Credit Growth
summary: PJT Partners Chair and CEO Paul Taubman says M&A activity isn't accelerating but at healthy levels. Speaking with Bloomberg's Dani Burger at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California, Taubman calls the private credit market’s issues a “public relations” challenge. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UBS' Abigail Watt Discusses Uncertainty in Labor Market
summary: Abigail Watt from UBS joined the program to discuss the current economic environment and its capacity to support higher prices. She highlighted that consumer spending is expected to remain somewhat stronger in the second quarter, although this outlook depends on the balance between rising gasoline prices and the impact of tax refunds. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Retail Investors Will Stop Fueling Private Credit Growth, PJT CEO Says
summary: PJT Partners Inc. Chief Executive Officer Paul Taubman said the private credit market’s issues are a “public relations” challenge as investors started to fully realize that many of the products weren’t fully liquid.

title: Sugar Hits One-Month High as Investors Unwind Short Positions
summary: Raw sugar futures rose to the highest in more than a month as investors trim bearish bets on expectations that mills at top supplier Brazil will focus on producing sugar-cane ethanol instead of the sweetener.

title: BofA Slate of a Dozen Directors Wins Approval From Investors
summary: Bank of America Corp. said shareholders on Monday approved a slate of 12 directors the company nominated for its board.

title: Khosla Warns Software Pain Will ‘Taint Everything’ Across Credit
summary: Strategic Value Partners founder Victor Khosla expects years of elevated defaults in credit markets and is preparing to take advantage of opportunities created by greater dispersion among private credit managers.

title: ECB Likely to Hike Rates with Capital Economics' McKeown
summary: Jennifer McKeown, chief global economist at Capital Economics, discussed the near-term outlook for central bank policy, highlighting that the European Central Bank appears more likely to raise interest rates soon compared to the Bank of England or the Federal Reserve. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Defense Firm CSG Plunges by Record After Short-Seller Report
summary: CSG NV fell the most since its January listing after a short-seller report questioned the defense industry supplier’s business model.

title: Oaktree's Panossian Warns of Building Credit Market Risks
summary: "It's a little bit of a head-scratcher as to why the markets are as robust as they are," Armen Panossian, co-CEO and head of performing credit at Oaktree, says. Speaking with Bloomberg's Dani Burger at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California, Panossian warns investors are underestimating the fundamental risks facing the credit market. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UAE Issues Missile Warning as Rising Tensions Risk US-Iran Truce
summary: The United Arab Emirates on Monday issued a missile threat warning for the first time since a ceasefire between the US and Iran took hold about a month ago, underscoring the fragility of diplomatic efforts to end a war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy supplies.

title: Gold Falls as Traders Assess Hormuz Tensions and Inflation Risks
summary: Gold fell, following its second weekly decline, as traders monitored progress toward a US-Iran deal and implications of heightened Middle East tensions on inflation and the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path.

title: Spirit Airlines Seeks to Start Orderly Wind-Down to Sell Assets
summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. is set to start an orderly wind-down process to sell its assets, capping the downfall of the low-cost carrier that has filed for bankruptcy twice in recent years and failed to clinch a last-minute US government rescue.

title: Portugal Tightens Citizenship Rules After Immigration Spike
summary: With Portugal’s foreign population reaching unprecedented levels, the country’s president has signed into law a bill that will double the amount of time needed for foreigners to obtain citizenship.

title: Bolivia Plots Global Bond Market Return After Four-Year Hiatus
summary: Bolivia is gauging investor interest for its first dollar bond sale in four years after the new market-friendly government avoided a default on external debt payments in March.

title: FedEx, UPS Shares Tumble on Amazon’s ‘Watershed’ Logistics Move
summary: US transportation stocks plunged Monday morning after Amazon.com Inc. announced expanded logistics offerings that will turn it into a major competitor for parcel carriers and air freight companies, and also impact truckers and third-party brokers.

title: UAE Says Swap Talks With US Are About Joining ‘Elite’ Group
summary: The United Arab Emirates signaled its currency swap talks with the US were about joining a small club with access to Federal Reserve liquidity lines rather than a need for external financing.

title: US Stocks Waver Near Record High With Mideast Tensions in Focus
summary: The S&P 500 Index fluctuated near a record high as investors monitored developments in the Middle East and geared up for another busy earnings stretch after solid results helped drive the gauge higher the past five weeks.

title: EU Power Markets Lowers Hourly Price Floor to -600 Euros
summary: European power markets are set to cut the hourly price limit to -€600 per megawatt-hour after an intense period of volatility where the existing floor price was reached a number of times.

title: Apollo's Zito on Fears Facing Private Credit Investors
summary: Apollo Asset Management co-President John Zito talks about how AI is impacting investing and private credit. “Everyone is acknowledging we will be in a higher-volatility regime, but they are not acknowledging credit is actually the typical safer place to be," he told Bloomberg's Dani Burger at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: These Are the Players in the Million-Dollar Dinosaur Fossil Business
summary:
Why are dinosaur bones joining the collections of millionaires and billionaires like Ken Griffin?

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, we are joined by Salomon Aaron, a director at London-based gallery David Aaron. He tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway how the private market for fossils actually works and how similar it is to the market for art and other antiquities. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Cruise Ship in Suspected Deadly Outbreak Stuck Off Cape Verde
summary: A cruise ship carrying 149 passengers and crew suspected of harboring a rare respiratory virus that may have caused three deaths is stuck near Cape Verde as health authorities rush to investigate.

title: Mexico Exchange Biva Sees ‘Serious’ Investor Interest in Bidding
summary: Mexico’s second-largest stock exchange, Biva, has received “serious” interest from potential bidders in a sale process that kicked off at the end of last year, said Chief Executive Officer Maria Ariza.

title: Oil Rises on Signs of Heightened Tensions in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Oil rose on signs of heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran attacked a tanker with drones and the US said it’s working to evacuate stranded ships through the vital waterway.

title: Surprise Rate Hold Upends Colombia’s Markets Weeks Before Vote
summary: Colombia’s peso and short-term rates plunged on Monday following the central bank’s unexpected decision to hold borrowing costs just weeks before a pivotal election.

title: Baltimore’s Mayor on Why the City Is More Than What You See on TV
summary:
Since Mayor Brandon Scott first took office in 2020, he's made it his mission to fix Baltimore's vacant housing crisis and make room for usable housing supply.

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Mayor Scott tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway about the historic effects of gun violence and why Baltimore natives aren't huge fans of The Wire. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump-Linked World Liberty Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
summary: World Liberty Financial said it is suing Justin Sun for defamation, accusing the billionaire crypto entrepreneur of running a coordinated media campaign against the Trump-linked project after he filed his own lawsuit against it last month.
